meta.display_name: Assess deal risks for an enterprise renewal with public-company context

task: You are a deal desk analyst reviewing the Salesforce renewal for `agent@weaverenterprises.com`. Read the inbox to understand the current state of the deal. Then look up the counterparty's public filings using SEC EDGAR. Produce exactly two markdown sections: `## Deal Risk Assessment` and `## Recommended Actions`. In `## Deal Risk Assessment`, include these labeled fields: `Account:`, `Ticker:`, `Deal Value:`, `Renewal Deadline:`, `Risk Level:` (High/Medium/Low), and `Risk Factors:` (a bulleted list of at least 3 specific risks from the emails). In `## Recommended Actions`, include at least 4 prioritized bullets formatted as `- [P1] ...`, `- [P2] ...`, or `- [P3] ...`. Include one `Public Company Context:` line with factual data from SEC EDGAR filings. Base every claim on tool output.

seed_emails.subject: Salesforce renewal status update
seed_emails.body_text: Quick update on the Salesforce renewal. Deal value is $2.4M ARR, renewal deadline is April 15. Champion is their VP of IT, Marcus Chen. The procurement team has been slow to respond and we still have not received the signed SOW. I am worried we may slip past the deadline. They also asked for a 12% discount which is above our 10% cap.

seed_emails.subject: Salesforce MSA redlines outstanding
seed_emails.body_text: Legal update on Salesforce. Three redlines remain open: 1) indemnification cap — they want unlimited, we cap at 2x deal value, 2) data residency — they require EU data storage which we do not yet offer, 3) audit rights — they want annual on-site audits which conflicts with our standard remote audit clause. I recommend we escalate the data residency issue to product since it requires infrastructure work.

seed_emails.subject: Salesforce security review pending
seed_emails.body_text: Their security team has not yet approved our SOC 2 Type II report. They flagged two findings: 1) our incident response SLA of 72 hours does not meet their 24-hour requirement, 2) they want evidence of annual penetration testing which we completed in January but have not shared the report. I can send the pentest report this week but the incident response SLA gap needs product and ops alignment.

seed_emails.subject: Salesforce usage and adoption metrics
seed_emails.body_text: Sharing Salesforce adoption data for the renewal. Current active users: 3,200 out of 4,000 seats (80% adoption). API call volume has increased 40% quarter over quarter. Three power users from their data team filed a feature request for custom dashboards last month. NPS score from the last survey was 42, down from 51 last quarter. The drop was driven by complaints about report generation speed.

seed_emails.subject: Salesforce deal economics
seed_emails.body_text: Finance review of the Salesforce renewal terms. Current contract is $2.1M. Proposed uplift to $2.4M includes premium support tier and 500 additional seats. Their 12% discount ask would bring it to $2.11M, below our floor of $2.16M (10% cap). Gross margin on this account is 74% so there is some room but not at 12%. Recommend holding at 8% max discount and bundling the premium support to justify the price.

seed_emails.subject: Salesforce exec alignment needed
seed_emails.body_text: I spoke with Salesforce's CRO yesterday. They are evaluating a competitor (ServiceNow) for part of the workflow. This is not a full replacement risk but could reduce our seat count by 800 if they move forward. We need to demonstrate value in the next two weeks or they will split the contract. Recommend an exec briefing with their CTO before the renewal deadline.
